stem: In triangle ABC, the measure of angle A is 38° and the measure of angle B is 72°. In triangle PQR, the measure of angle P is 38° and the measure of angle Q is 72°. Which of the following additional pieces of information, if any, is needed to determine whether triangle ABC is similar to triangle PQR?

answerOptions.content: The measures of both angle C and angle R

rationale: Choice A is correct. By the angle-angle (AA) similarity criterion, if two angles of one triangle are congruent to two angles of another triangle, the triangles are similar. Here angle A is congruent to angle P (both 38°) and angle B is congruent to angle Q (both 72°), so triangles ABC and PQR are similar without further information. Choices B, C, and D are incorrect because the third pair of angles can be determined from the triangle angle-sum theorem and need not be given.
